summary: Follow-up automation and hygiene gaps found while reconciling llm_wiki_vnext_code_review_report_260422.md against the current repository state.

observations.problem: The current runtime has started adding dataclass and TypedDict seams, but many schema-backed artifacts are still assembled and passed internally as raw dict payloads.
observations.why_it_matters: Schema validation catches boundary drift late, while raw dict assembly still leaves refactors exposed to misspelled fields, implicit optional fields, and fixture-heavy tests.
observations.automation_candidate: Introduce a small typed artifact-model layer for the hottest payload families, starting with session loop state, run telemetry, workspace preparation telemetry, promotion report inputs, and filesystem apply reports.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-23 KST for the current auto-improve queue tranche by modeling MutationProposal, PriorityBreakdown, QueueSelectionDiagnostics, ReadinessQueue, and ReadinessRemediation as typed runtime payloads with schema-backed dict conversion only at to_wire/report boundaries. Keep migrating one artifact family at a time behind builder functions or TypedDict/dataclass request objects, prioritizing session loop state, run telemetry, workspace preparation telemetry, and filesystem apply reports before expanding mypy-strict-preview.

observations.problem: Structural complexity reporting exists, but the default monitored profiles do not directly track several high-complexity functions highlighted by the current code scan and external report.
observations.why_it_matters: High-branch helper functions can stay outside the touched-surface budget unless a run modifies them, so complexity debt can remain visible in ad hoc reviews but absent from the regular preview artifact.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a dedicated high-complexity-helper profile or generate one from python_function_review candidates, then include per-function top-N diagnostics in the structural complexity budget report.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-23 KST by adding the high_complexity_helpers preview profile and diagnostics.function_budget_top_n monitored/unmonitored function ranking. Updated on 2026-04-23 KST by decomposing `ops/scripts/raw_intake_promotion_runtime.py` into shared/bridge/scaffold/validation helper modules and `ops/scripts/observability_artifacts_runtime.py` into shared schema write plus artifact fingerprint, decision-metrics, and routing-provenance families, so the monitored helper runtime surfaces now return to pass in `make complexity-budget`. Updated again on 2026-04-23 KST by adding the current hotspot watchlist to the default critical runtime orchestrator profile and splitting the long readiness, mutation-proposal, and outcome-metrics calibration builders below the function-budget threshold. Extended on 2026-04-23 KST with diagnostics.function_budget_monitoring so unmonitored function-budget candidates now produce a preview attention signal even when the top-level monitored target profile is otherwise quiet. Keep the profile preview-only until the expanded hotspot list remains quiet across normal maintenance runs.

observations.problem: Repo-internal writers now have resolve_repo_output_path, but the older resolve_output_path and resolve_vault_path helpers still intentionally allow absolute paths for user-facing export or normalization tools.
observations.why_it_matters: The distinction is useful, but without a writer classification test future operational artifact writers can accidentally choose the permissive helper and write outside the repo.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a small registry or static test that classifies each CLI writer as repo_artifact or user_export and asserts it calls the appropriate output path resolver.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-23 KST by adding a static writer classification guard in tests/test_writer_output_paths.py. Keep absolute paths available for explicit user-export, archive, raw-normalization, and curation/scaffold surfaces, but require new repo artifact writers to stay classified and avoid resolve_output_path.
